private.name: Tenna Systems

data.narrative:
Distributed Spectrum is a Seed-stage Defense & Security company that has raised $25M to date. The valuation analysis draws from a peer universe of 10 comparable companies: 2 public (DRSHF, IDEAFORGE.NS) and 8 private firms including Anduril Industries, Shield AI, Tenna Systems, and Saronic. The median peer valuation across same-sector and adjacent-stage companies is $150M, with a range of $60M to $500M.

Two triangulation methods were applied. Stage-progression analysis uses the $25M raised as a proxy valuation and applies a 2.5x step-up multiplier typical for Seed-to-next-stage progression, yielding $62M. Peer-median analysis examines five Defense & Security peers spanning Seed and Series A stages—Tenna Systems ($60M), NODA AI ($125M), Ares Industries ($150M), Vatn Systems ($300M), and Stark Defence ($500M)—producing a $150M implied valuation. The two methods are weighted equally.

The triangulated analysis supports a valuation range of $53M (low) to $173M (high), with a median of $106M. Key uncertainties include relative positioning within the Defense & Security sector relative to Series A-stage peers, revenue traction and differentiation versus the broader peer set, and customer concentration in what is typically a concentrated, contract-dependent market.
